commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Telegram prompt-context projection so cached reply messages can use already-hydrated reply-chain media paths instead of `telegram:file/<file_id>` pseudo-refs, and updates the focused Telegram bot regression test.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +1. Total +19 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR blocker by source review: a hydrated media map built from the reply chain is applied to conversation-context entries without evidence that it is limited to the visible reply-chain subset used by Telegram group visibility filtering. The original bug also has focused harness proof in the PR body, but no live Telegram proof yet.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow automated repair can constrain the media-ref override to visible Telegram reply-chain entries and add the missing regression; live transport proof still needs a maintainer or contributor after the repair.

target.title: fix(telegram): expose hydrated media refs in prompt context
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes Telegram reply-image prompt context for #87401.

- Telegram reply-chain context can keep `telegram:file/<file_id>` as plugin-internal metadata.
- Model-facing structured conversation context now uses the already-hydrated reply-chain `mediaPath` when that reply media was resolved for the current message.
- The change is intentionally scoped to Telegram prompt-context projection in `extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts`; it does not change message-cache persistence or teach the core image tool to understand Telegram file IDs.

## Linked context

Closes #87401

Related media-store/image-tool work: #87024, #87101.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Requested from the local Codex maintainer workflow for #87401.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Telegram reply-chain photo context no longer exposes an unsupported `telegram:file/<file_id>` reference to the model/image-tool path when that reply photo has already been downloaded and hydrated for the current message.
- Real environment tested: local macOS OpenClaw source worktree on PR head `54d917c199`, Node/Vitest runtime, Telegram handler runtime exercised through the repo Telegram harness, and temporary OpenClaw media files created by the handler during the focused proof command.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'`; `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main`.
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from the current simplified branch showed `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 1 passed | 71 skipped (72)` for the focused Telegram handler proof. The regression assertion verifies structured context for reply-target message `101` uses `payload.ReplyChain[1].mediaPath` and explicitly rejects `telegram:file/generated-photo-1` as the model-facing `media_ref`.
- Observed result after fix: the prompt-context projection uses the hydrated reply-chain media path only when the current reply-chain payload already has one, while preserving `telegram:file/generated-photo-1` as internal reply-chain metadata.
- What was not tested: live Telegram Desktop/Bot API proof was not completed from this fork-owned PR branch. The attempted `@openclaw-mantis telegram desktop proof` run `26553623006` stopped at authorization with `Actor MonkeyLeeT permission: read`, and direct workflow dispatch returned `HTTP 403: Must have admin rights to Repository`.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this is accurate local runtime harness proof for the narrowed code path, but it is not the live Telegram visual proof ClawSweeper asked for; a maintainer-owned Mantis trigger or proof override is still needed if live transport proof remains required.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the fix, the focused regression observed `media_ref: "telegram:file/generated-photo-1"` in structured conversation context.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts -t 'hydrates bot reply targets cached from earlier Telegram replies'`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Updated the Telegram bot reply-target hydration regression to assert model-facing structured context receives the hydrated reply-chain media path and not `telegram:file/generated-photo-1`.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The focused regression failed before the production change with `media_ref: "telegram:file/generated-photo-1"` in structured conversation context.

If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Telegram reply-image context exposed to the agent changes from an unsupported Telegram pseudo-URI to an image-tool-readable local media path when reply-chain media is already hydrated.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Model-facing Telegram structured context for hydrated reply-chain media.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The override only comes from the current `ReplyChain` entries produced by the existing reply-media hydration path. If no hydrated reply-chain path exists, the existing cached `mediaRef` fallback remains unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Draft PR is ready for maintainer review of the simplified diff. Live Mantis Telegram Desktop proof requires a maintainer-owned trigger because this fork author cannot dispatch the secret-bearing workflow.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI is rerunning on the simplified branch. Maintainers may still require live Telegram proof or apply a proof override.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Simplified the branch back to the narrow reply-chain projection fix so reviewers no longer have to review the reverted cache persistence/recent-media scope.
